note_data.title: Transport
keyPoints: Transport is the movement of substances from one place to another in living organisms
Plants have two main transport systems: Xylem (transports water and minerals upward) and Phloem (transports prepared food)
Transpiration is the loss of water in the form of water vapors from aerial parts of plants, mainly through stomata
Root pressure and transpiration pull are the two main forces responsible for water movement in plants
Blood is the main transport medium in humans consisting of plasma, RBCs, WBCs and platelets
Heart is a muscular organ with four chambers - two atria and two ventricles
Single circulation occurs in fish while double circulation occurs in mammals including humans
Arteries carry blood away from heart, veins carry blood toward heart, capillaries connect arteries and veins
Lymphatic system helps in drainage of tissue fluid and defense against diseases

definitions.definition: The movement of substances from one place to another in living organisms
The loss of water in the form of water vapors from aerial parts of plants, mainly through stomata
The pressure developed in roots due to active absorption of water and minerals from soil
A liquid connective tissue that transports substances throughout the body
A colorless fluid containing lymphocytes that bathes the tissues and drains through lymphatic vessels

importantQuestions.question: Differentiate between arteries and veins (any 3 points)
importantQuestions.answer: Arteries: carry blood away from heart, have thick muscular walls, no valves (except pulmonary). Veins: carry blood toward heart, have thin walls, have valves to prevent backflow

importantQuestions.question: Describe the structure and function of human heart
importantQuestions.answer: Heart is a muscular organ with 4 chambers - 2 atria (right and left) and 2 ventricles (right and left). Right atrium receives deoxygenated blood from body, right ventricle pumps it to lungs. Left atrium receives oxygenated blood from lungs, left ventricle pumps it to body. Valves prevent backflow - tricuspid between right atrium and ventricle, bicuspid between left atrium and ventricle. Heart wall has 3 layers: epicardium, myocardium, endocardium. Function is to pump blood throughout body maintaining circulation

mcqs.question: Which tissue transports water and minerals in plants?
How many chambers are present in human heart?

commonMistakes: Students confuse xylem and phloem functions - remember xylem carries water UP, phloem carries food
Writing veins carry oxygenated blood - remember veins carry deoxygenated blood except pulmonary vein
Forgetting to mention stomata when defining transpiration
Confusing single and double circulation - fish have single, mammals have double
Not drawing proper labels in heart diagram - always label all 4 chambers and major vessels
examTips: Heart diagram appears in every BSEK paper - practice drawing with proper labels
Transport chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question worth 8 marks
Memorize exact definitions of transpiration, root pressure and blood from textbook
Differentiation questions between arteries/veins and xylem/phloem are very common
Always mention Pakistani examples like Indus River for transport in geography context
note_data.keyDiagram: Structure of Human Heart showing 4 chambers (right atrium, left atrium, right ventricle, left ventricle), valves (tricuspid, bicuspid), and major blood vessels (aorta, pulmonary artery, pulmonary vein, vena cava). Label direction of blood flow with arrows.
